title: Multi-Agent Systems Path
description: Build fluency in Multi-Agent Systems inside Reinforcement Learning by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning
steps.summary: A framework for reinforcement learning that involves multiple agents interacting with each other. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning / Multi-Agent Systems. Start after Reinforcement Learning Agent and Learning Agent. It opens into Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) and Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) for Control.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Competitive Learning
steps.summary: Competitive Learning belongs to the Reinforcement Learning area of AI/ML, within Multi-Agent Systems. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning / Multi-Agent Systems. Start after Q-Learning and V-Learning.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Hierarchical Multiagent Systems
steps.summary: Hierarchical Multiagent Systems belongs to the Reinforcement Learning area of AI/ML, within Multi-Agent Systems. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning / Multi-Agent Systems. Start after Multiagent Systems and Hierarchical Networks.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: AI in Cooperative AI

steps.title: Multiagent Systems
steps.summary: Multiagent Systems belongs to the Reinforcement Learning area of AI/ML, within Multi-Agent Systems. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning / Multi-Agent Systems. Compare it with Hierarchical Multiagent Systems and Cooperative MARL. It opens into Hierarchical Multiagent Systems.
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.

steps.title: Competitive Agents
